metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC3 verify command (or add a separate verify line) to confirm the architecture.md remedy row is absent, e.g. `! grep 'inbound_differ\|inbound_status' docs/architecture.md`.
evidence: AC3 text: 'its `docs/architecture.md` remedy row is deleted'
AC3 verify: '`! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`' — architecture.md not included
findings.finding: AC3's verify command (`! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`) covers only the two allowlist files, but the AC's textual claim also requires deleting the stale remedy row in `docs/architecture.md`. The architecture.md deletion has no proving command, so an executor could skip it and still satisfy the stated verify command.
scenarios: An agent runs the verify command, it passes (the allowlist files are clean), but the stale architecture.md row remains, leaving documentation debt undetected.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: `(verify: ! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt && ! grep -n 'inbound_differ' docs/architecture.md)`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3's verify command to also check that the stale remedy row is gone from docs/architecture.md (e.g. `! grep -n 'inbound_differ\|inbound_status' docs/architecture.md` or a dedicated grep line).
evidence: AC3 text: 'its docs/architecture.md remedy row is deleted'
AC3 verify command: '! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt' — architecture.md is absent from this grep
What section: 'DELETES the stale row as part of graduating the file'
findings.finding: AC3's verify command does not check the `docs/architecture.md` deletion that the same AC line explicitly names as a deliverable, leaving that step unverifiable from the acceptance criteria alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC line 3 verify step
scenarios: An implementer removes the allowlist rows (passing the grep verify) but forgets to delete the architecture.md row; all verify commands pass but the stated deliverable is incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3's verify: `&& ! grep 'inbound_status\.py' docs/architecture.md`

findings.finding: AC3's verify command (`! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`) does not check docs/architecture.md, even though the AC text explicitly requires the remedy row in that file to be deleted. The docs/architecture.md deletion is claimed but has no proving command.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the verify command to: `! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt docs/architecture.md`

findings.finding: AC3's verify command (`! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`) does not verify the third action the criterion's own text describes: deletion of the remedy row in `docs/architecture.md`. The criterion's text names three artifacts to clean up, but its verify step only checks two.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3 verify: `&& ! grep 'inbound_differ' docs/architecture.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `inbound_differ.py` imports back from `inbound_fields.py` using the same `spec_from_file_location`/lazy-load pattern already used for `adf.py` and `link_direction.py` (not a package import), so the file remains loadable in the spec-loaded test context the entire reconciler test suite relies on.
evidence: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_inbound_differ.py:25: `INBOUND_DIFFER_PATH = ENGINE / 'inbound_differ.py'` loaded via `_load_module('inbound_differ', INBOUND_DIFFER_PATH)`
tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_inbound_differ_field_contract.py:27: path-based load of inbound_differ.py
tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/conflict/test_status_annotation_labels.py:36: path-based load of inbound_differ.py

The existing `inbound_differ.py` itself uses `_load_adf()` via `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` precisely to avoid package-context dependency — the same pattern must apply to the inbound_fields import
Plan does not specify the import mechanism for `inbound_differ.py` -> `inbound_fields.py`
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `inbound_differ.py` will 'import back from `inbound_fields`' the moved symbols, but every test in the reconciler suite loads `inbound_differ.py` via `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` (a path-based load that gives no package context). A regular `from rebar_reconciler.inbound_fields import ...` or relative `from . import inbound_fields` inside the spec-loaded `inbound_differ.py` will raise `ImportError` / `ModuleNotFoundError` in these isolated test contexts, breaking the entire reconciler test suite despite AC5's claimed oracle.
findings.location: ## What — 'inbound_differ.py imports these back from inbound_fields'; AC5 — 'pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler -q'
scenarios: All tests that spec-load inbound_differ.py directly (the majority of the reconciler test suite) fail immediately with ImportError when inbound_differ.py tries to `from rebar_reconciler.inbound_fields import ...` because the spec-loaded module has no package context.

The existing lazy-load pattern (importlib.util.spec_from_file_location for adf.py and link_direction.py) is the established workaround — the plan must specify that the same pattern is used for inbound_fields, but it doesn't.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `_load_inbound_fields()` lazy loader in `inbound_differ.py` mirroring `_load_adf()`, then use it to access moved symbols; or add `inbound_fields.py` to `sys.modules` before its first use. Document this in the 'What' section so the implementer doesn't use a bare package import.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `_load_adf` split-seam conflict: either (a) add `_load_adf` to the re-export shim list in AC6 so inbound_differ.py re-imports it from inbound_fields, (b) keep a duplicate `_load_adf` loader in inbound_differ.py (as the other sibling modules do), or (c) clarify whether `_load_adf` should stay rather than move.
evidence: Plan MOVE list: '_load_adf (+ _ADF_KEY_INBOUND, _AdfModule_Inbound)'
Plan STAY list: '_diff_jira_vs_local' (does not mention _load_adf)
AC6 re-export list: '_map_jira_to_local_fields, _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS, _assignee_matches, _normalize_jira_body' — _load_adf is absent
inbound_differ.py line 328: 'local_val = _load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit(local_val)' inside _diff_jira_vs_local
findings.finding: _load_adf (listed as MOVE to inbound_fields.py) is called directly from _diff_jira_vs_local (listed as STAY in inbound_differ.py) at line 328. After the split, inbound_differ.py would reference an undefined name unless _load_adf is either kept in inbound_differ.py or added to the re-export shim. AC6's re-export list does not include _load_adf.
findings.location: What section / moved symbols list; AC6 re-export list; inbound_differ.py line 328
scenarios: After the split, inbound_differ.py is imported; _diff_jira_vs_local is called; _load_adf is not defined in that module scope → NameError at runtime.
Test suite (test_inbound_differ_field_contract.py, test_inbound_differ.py, etc.) exercises _diff_jira_vs_local via compute_inbound_mutations → all fail with NameError.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep a local _load_adf in inbound_differ.py (matching the pattern in outbound_fields.py and outbound_comments.py, which each own an independent lazy loader) and move only the translation-cluster symbols that have no callers in the staying code. Alternatively, add _load_adf to AC6's re-export list and import it back explicitly in inbound_differ.py.

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_differ.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_differ.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt` greps the unanchored word 'inbound_differ' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_inbound_differ'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"inbound_differ":')
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command to AC6 that actually imports the re-exported symbols via `inbound_differ`, e.g. `python -c "from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import inbound_differ; inbound_differ._map_jira_to_local_fields; inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS; inbound_differ._assignee_matches; inbound_differ._normalize_jira_body"`
evidence: AC6 text ends with '(re-exported)' — no verify: clause present
All other ACs carry an explicit `(verify: <command>)` inline; AC6 does not
findings.finding: AC6 states a concrete backward-compatibility outcome ('still resolve as module attributes (re-exported)') but provides no proving command. An executor has no deterministic in-session check to confirm the re-exports actually work at import time.
scenarios: An executor adds the re-export lines but makes a typo (e.g., imports from the wrong name), and nothing in the suite catches it because the reconciler tests happen to import directly from inbound_fields rather than via inbound_differ. The AC passes on subjective inspection only.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC6: `(verify: python -c "from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import inbound_differ; _ = (inbound_differ._map_jira_to_local_fields, inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS, inbound_differ._assignee_matches, inbound_differ._normalize_jira_body)")`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a short explanatory comment block in `inbound_differ.py` (and/or an AGENTS.md note) marking the re-exported private symbols as an intentional backward-compat shim so future maintainers do not remove them as dead code.
evidence: 'inbound_differ.py imports these back from inbound_fields and RE-EXPORTS them (so inbound_differ.<symbol> attribute access and the config/parity tests keep resolving)'

AC: 'Backward compatibility preserved: inbound_differ._map_jira_to_local_fields, inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS, inbound_differ._assignee_matches, inbound_differ._normalize_jira_body still resolve as module attributes (re-exported)'
ABSENCE: no instruction to add a comment block or AGENTS.md note explaining why the re-exports must not be removed
findings.finding: The re-export shim pattern (private `_`-prefixed symbols re-exported from `inbound_differ` for backward compat) is an unusual convention that is validated by an AC check but not captured in any durable comment, AGENTS.md note, or architecture doc. A future maintainer scanning `inbound_differ.py` may remove the apparently redundant re-exports as dead code, silently breaking any consumer that imports via `inbound_differ.<symbol>`.
findings.location: ## What — re-export paragraph; Acceptance Criteria — AC line 6
scenarios: A developer performing a future cleanup pass sees private symbols imported and immediately re-exported in inbound_differ.py, infers they are unused, and deletes them — breaking config/parity tests or any consumer that does `from inbound_differ import _map_jira_to_local_fields`.
findings.suggested_fix:
Immediately above the re-export lines in `inbound_differ.py`, add:
```python
# Backward-compat re-exports: symbols moved to inbound_fields.py.
# Config and parity tests import these via `inbound_differ.<symbol>`;
# do not remove without updating those consumers.
from ._inbound_fields import (  # noqa: F401
    _load_adf, _ADF_KEY_INBOUND, _AdfModule_Inbound,
    _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_TYPE, _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_PRIORITY,
    _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS, _REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL,
    _extract_jira_field_value, _assignee_matches,
    _normalize_jira_body, _map_jira_to_local_fields,
)
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete verification step to AC #6 (e.g., a `python -c` import smoke-test or a new assertion in `test_module_size_contract.py`) that confirms the named symbols still resolve as attributes of `inbound_differ` after the split.
evidence: Every other AC has a parenthetical verify command, e.g. '(verify: wc -l ...)' or '(verify: pytest ...)', but AC #6 lists only the required symbol names with no verification step.
The plan notes 'the config/parity tests keep resolving' but does not cite a specific test file or add a new assertion to guarantee it.
Absence: no `python -c 'from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import inbound_differ; inbound_differ._map_jira_to_local_fields'` or equivalent smoke-test is specified.
findings.finding: AC #6 (backward-compat re-exports) names a requirement but provides no end-state-observable proof: unlike every other AC, it has no `verify:` command and no test assertion confirming that `inbound_differ._map_jira_to_local_fields` et al. still resolve as module attributes after the split. An implementer could omit the re-exports and the reconciler test suite might still pass if those tests import from `inbound_fields` rather than `inbound_differ`.
scenarios: An implementer adds the new module but forgets the re-exports in inbound_differ.py; the reconciler unit tests (which import directly) still pass; the backward-compat break goes undetected until a downstream caller (config or parity test importing via inbound_differ) fails at runtime.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC #6: `(verify: python -c 'from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import inbound_differ; assert hasattr(inbound_differ, "_map_jira_to_local_fields") and hasattr(inbound_differ, "_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS") and hasattr(inbound_differ, "_assignee_matches") and hasattr(inbound_differ, "_normalize_jira_body")')` or add a parametrized test in `test_module_size_contract.py` that imports and asserts each re-exported name.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'grep finds neither' assertion: `_jira_status_to_local` exists in `inbound_translate.py:126`; clarify whether the plan's intent is that it doesn't exist *in inbound_differ.py* (which is true), and reconcile whether `inbound_fields.py` will duplicate translation maps already present in `inbound_translate.py` (`_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL`, `_JIRA_TYPE_MAP`, `_JIRA_PRIORITY_MAP`, `_normalize_adf_body`).
evidence: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py:126: `def _jira_status_to_local(jira_status: str) -> str:`
src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py:123 imports `_jira_status_to_local` from inbound_translate
tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_idea_status_translation.py:27: `assert inbound_translate._jira_status_to_local('IDEA') == 'idea'`
Plan quote: 'verified: grep finds neither' — the grep for `_jira_status_to_local` returns multiple hits
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'grep finds neither' for both `inbound_status.py` AND `_jira_status_to_local`, but `_jira_status_to_local` DOES exist in the codebase at `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py:126`. The unqualified grep claim is false — the function exists, just in a different file than the stale architecture.md remedy row prescribed.
findings.location: ## Why — 'Note on the architecture.md record: the offenders-table remedy row for this file is STALE — it names a target module `inbound_status.py` and a function `_jira_status_to_local` that do NOT exist in the current code (verified: grep finds neither)'
scenarios: The false premise ('_jira_status_to_local does not exist') is used to justify deleting the architecture.md remedy row as 'stale'. If a reviewer trusts this premise, they may not notice that `inbound_translate.py` already partially implements what the remedy row described, and the relationship between the plan's new `inbound_fields.py` and the existing `inbound_translate.py` (which has parallel/duplicate translation maps) is left unexamined.
findings.suggested_fix: Narrow the claim: 'the architecture.md remedy row names `inbound_status.py` and a function `_jira_status_to_local` that do NOT exist *in inbound_differ.py*'. Then explicitly address whether `inbound_fields.py` creates a third copy of translation maps that already exist in both `inbound_differ.py` and `inbound_translate.py`, or whether the split should instead merge the differ's copies into the existing `inbound_translate.py`.

findings.finding: The plan's proposed split seam (`inbound_fields.py`) diverges from the remedy row recorded in `docs/architecture.md`, which describes a different seam: extracting into `inbound_status.py` with `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` + `_jira_status_to_local and friends`. The function `_jira_status_to_local` does not exist in `inbound_differ.py` at all, confirming the architecture.md row is describing a different (apparently superseded or aspirational) split. The plan says to 'delete its remedy row' — which is correct — but this discrepancy means the plan should acknowledge the architectural record disagreement rather than silently deleting a row that points to a different target.
findings.location: ## What / plan claim 'docs/architecture.md already records' the seam; docs/architecture.md line 265
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence in the ## Why or ## Notes section: 'The architecture.md remedy row described an earlier seam (`inbound_status.py` / `_jira_status_to_local`); that seam was superseded — `_jira_status_to_local` was never extracted and the broader `inbound_fields.py` cluster is the correct seam. The remedy row is deleted (not updated) as part of graduation.'

findings.finding: The plan's positive rationale claims it splits 'on the call-graph seam docs/architecture.md already records,' but the architecture.md remedy row (line 265) prescribes a different module name ('inbound_status.py') and a narrower scope (status map + status-translation helpers only). The plan extracts a broader cluster (also _load_adf, _extract_jira_field_value, _assignee_matches, _normalize_jira_body, _map_jira_to_local_fields) into 'inbound_fields.py' without explaining why this wider cut and different name are preferred over the recorded seam.
findings.location: ## Why / ## What — rationale + symbol list; docs/architecture.md line 265

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `inbound_differ.py`'s import of `inbound_fields.py` must use the same `try: from rebar_reconciler._loader import lazy_load / except ImportError: bootstrap by file path` idiom used by `outbound_fields.py`, since all 25+ reconciler unit tests load `inbound_differ.py` via `spec_from_file_location` (no package context), making plain relative imports fail.
evidence: outbound_fields.py module docstring: 'Like the other reconciler modules, this module keeps its OWN lazy _load_adf loader (the differ may be spec-loaded by file path in tests, where `from . import` does not resolve)'
outbound_fields.py lines 37-49: try/except bootstrap of _loader.py for exactly this path-load context
25+ test files each contain `spec_from_file_location(..., INBOUND_DIFFER_PATH)` and call `spec.loader.exec_module(mod)` — no package context is established
test_config.py:191 calls `_load_attr('inbound_differ.py', '_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS')` which path-loads inbound_differ.py and calls getattr — if the import of inbound_fields fails, this assertion fails
Absence: the plan does not mention the `from . import` constraint or specify a lazy/bootstrap import idiom for the new inbound_fields dependency
findings.finding: The plan specifies that inbound_differ.py will 'import these back from inbound_fields' but does not specify the import mechanism. The entire test suite (25+ files) loads inbound_differ.py via `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(..., INBOUND_DIFFER_PATH)` — a path-based load that does NOT establish package context, making `from .inbound_fields import ...` or `from rebar_reconciler.inbound_fields import ...` fail at import time. The existing analogue (outbound_fields.py) documents this exact constraint in its module docstring and solves it with a `try: from rebar_reconciler._loader import lazy_load / except ImportError: bootstrap by file path` pattern. Without specifying the same idiom for the inbound_fields import, the 're-export for backward compatibility' mechanism will silently fail for all spec-loaded test fixtures, and AC6 (backward-compat attributes resolve) will not be satisfied by the test oracle the plan names (the reconciler test suite).
findings.location: ## What — 'inbound_differ.py imports these back from inbound_fields and RE-EXPORTS them'
scenarios: Any of the 25+ unit test fixtures that spec-load inbound_differ.py by path will hit an ImportError when inbound_differ.py tries `from .inbound_fields import ...`, breaking the entire reconciler test suite
test_config.py's parity test (`_load_attr('inbound_differ.py', '_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS')`) fails because the module can't be exec'd
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ## What section: 'inbound_differ.py must import inbound_fields using the project-standard lazy/bootstrap loader idiom (matching outbound_fields.py lines 37-49: try: from rebar_reconciler._loader import lazy_load / except ImportError: bootstrap by file path) — plain `from .inbound_fields import ...` will fail for all spec_from_file_location test fixtures.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py:191 as a consumer of the re-export chain and confirm (or add an AC note) that the `from rebar_reconciler.inbound_fields import _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` re-export works when inbound_differ.py is spec-loaded by absolute file path.
evidence: test_config.py line 178-185: _load_attr loads a module by file path via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location
test_config.py line 191: '_load_attr("inbound_differ.py", "_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS")["IDEA"] == "idea"'
Plan AC6: 'inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS still resolves as module attribute (re-exported)' — but this is verified only for package-import attribute access, not file-path spec-load
findings.finding: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py:191 spec-loads inbound_differ.py by file path (not as a package import) and reads _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS directly from the loaded module. After the split, _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS lives in inbound_fields.py and is only re-exported via a from-import in inbound_differ.py. The plan does not enumerate this consumer or confirm the re-export chain works under file-path spec-loading.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / AC6 re-export requirement; tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py line 191
scenarios: test_config.py:test_idea_maps_to_jira_idea_across_all_status_maps spec-loads inbound_differ.py in isolation; the re-export line 'from rebar_reconciler.inbound_fields import _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS' executes but may fail if rebar_reconciler.inbound_fields is not yet registered in sys.modules at that point.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
